prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `dc2e9a9d`.
codex_response:
Rule: each green object is a hollow rectangular frame with one centered tab on exactly one side. The output keeps the green object, adds a mirrored copy of the whole object on the opposite side with a one-cell gap, colors left/right-tab copies as `1`, colors top/bottom-tab copies as `8`, and clips any out-of-bounds part.

I corrected both hint sources. `opus46` had the mirror idea but was wrong about dropping the tab; `sonnet45` was too vague and effectively missed the per-object rule. That correction is recorded in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_dc2e9a9d/notes.md). No `arc2/dsl_ext.py` primitive was needed. The generator samples sparse 2-4 object scenes from the same tabbed-frame family, guarantees both horizontal and vertical cases, and spaces full original/copy footprints apart so the outputs stay on-family.

Verification passed: official examples `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`. I also did a terminal sanity check on the generated preview sheet.
